title: Buy a home
description: A composable home-buying checklist with generic and jurisdiction-specific overlays.
disclaimer: Educational checklist only. Real-estate, lending, tax, insurance, inspection, legal, and local requirements vary. Verify current requirements with licensed professionals and the cited public agencies.

tasks.title: Confirm why buying is preferable to renting for the expected occupancy period
Define target property type, bedrooms, accessibility, commute, schools, neighborhood, and future-space needs
Set a maximum all-in monthly housing cost, not only a purchase price
Create reserves for closing, moving, immediate repairs, furnishings, and emergencies
Identify ownership, immigration, trust, estate, or co-buyer questions requiring professional advice
Review credit reports and correct material errors
Gather income, employment, asset, debt, tax, identity, and source-of-funds documents
Avoid opening new credit or making unexplained large transfers during underwriting
Compare multiple lenders, loan products, rates, points, fees, and lock terms
Verify down-payment, reserve, gift-fund, and assistance-program rules
Compare listings against requirements and total ownership cost
Visit at different times and evaluate noise, traffic, lighting, parking, drainage, and nearby uses
Review seller disclosures, title materials, surveys, permits, warranties, and available maintenance records
Order an independent general inspection and attend when possible
Add specialist inspections when indicated, including roof, foundation, sewer, electrical, HVAC, plumbing, pests, environmental hazards, pool, chimney, or solar
Estimate near-term repairs, replacements, insurance constraints, and maintenance costs
Review HOA documents, finances, insurance, litigation, rules, assessments, and rental restrictions when applicable
Select offer price and terms using comparable sales, condition, competition, and affordability
Understand deposit, financing, appraisal, inspection, title, document-review, sale-of-home, and insurance contingencies
Track every contract deadline and responsible party
Verify wire instructions through a trusted known channel before sending funds
Complete underwriting requests promptly and preserve copies
Review appraisal results and resolve valuation or condition issues
Review preliminary title findings, ownership vesting, liens, easements, restrictions, and exceptions
Bind acceptable homeowners insurance before the lender deadline
Review final loan and settlement documents and reconcile cash to close
Perform a documented final walk-through and confirm agreed repairs and included items
Confirm recording, funding, keys, access devices, warranties, and possession
Store closing, loan, title, inspection, insurance, tax, warranty, and repair records securely
Transfer utilities, change locks and access codes, test safety devices, and document meter readings
Create a preventive-maintenance calendar and prioritize safety-critical repairs
Update address, insurance inventories, emergency contacts, and applicable registrations
Obtain and compare standardized Loan Estimates from multiple lenders
Review the Closing Disclosure in advance and resolve discrepancies before signing
Check eligibility and tradeoffs for conventional, FHA, VA, USDA, and approved assistance programs
Verify fair-housing and equal-credit protections and document suspected discrimination
For pre-1978 housing, review the federal lead-based-paint disclosure and available records
Check FEMA flood information and lender flood-insurance requirements

CFPB — Shopping for a mortgage
CFPB — Closing on your new home

sections.title: California disclosures, escrow, title, and tax context

tasks.title: Review the California agency-relationship disclosure and confirm who each licensee represents
Review the Transfer Disclosure Statement and investigate disclosed defects or conditions
Review the Natural Hazard Disclosure and independently evaluate applicable fire, flood, earthquake-fault, seismic, and other mapped risks
Review preliminary title, easements, liens, CC&Rs, and owner-versus-lender title insurance coverage
Verify the escrow holder, instructions, fee allocation, and conditions for release of funds and recording
For a new subdivision or common-interest development, review applicable public report and governing documents
Estimate base and voter-approved property taxes, special assessments, Mello-Roos or community-facilities-district charges
Confirm availability and cost of homeowners, wildfire, earthquake, flood, and other desired insurance before removing insurance-related contingencies
Verify retrofit, smoke-alarm, carbon-monoxide, water-heater, defensible-space, or point-of-sale requirements applicable to the property

sources.title: California DRE — Information for Homebuyers

sections.title: Contra Costa County records and property-tax checks

tasks.title: Verify parcel number, current assessment, tax-rate-area details, exemptions, and available property characteristics with the Assessor
Review the current secured tax bill, direct assessments, delinquency status, and payment schedule
Budget separately for one or more supplemental property-tax bills after purchase
Confirm the deed is recorded and ownership and mailing records are updated after closing
Apply for the homeowner’s exemption when eligible and track filing deadlines shown by the Assessor
Do not assume a mortgage impound account will pay supplemental tax bills; verify each bill directly
Check county hazard, environmental-health, sewer, septic, well, and permit records when relevant to the parcel

sources.title: Contra Costa County — New Property Owners
Contra Costa County — Reassessment
Contra Costa County — Tax FAQs
Contra Costa County Assessor
Contra Costa County Clerk-Recorder

tasks.title: Confirm the property is inside San Ramon city limits and identify its zoning or applicable specific plan
Review permit history and investigate additions, conversions, solar, pools, electrical, plumbing, HVAC, retaining walls, and other work for permit and final status
For Dougherty Valley property, verify the development-specific zoning standards, design guidelines, HOA rules, and applicable service providers
Identify the correct water and sewer providers for the exact address; San Ramon addresses may be served by different districts
Verify garbage, electricity, gas, internet, and other move-in service arrangements
Check mapped wildfire exposure, evacuation access, vegetation responsibilities, and insurance implications for the specific parcel
Evaluate commute routes, traffic patterns, noise sources, nearby development proposals, and planned land-use changes
Verify school assignments and enrollment directly with the school district rather than relying on listing descriptions
Check whether planned improvements require city approval and validate feasibility before treating them as usable space or future value
